Resource Name: Systemic Change Initiative - Getting Ahead Program
Resource Description: Holistic support classes to empower people to make their way out of poverty and into self-sufficiency and increase resiliency
Eligibility: Must reside within Houston-Galveston area and agree to attend all classes

Resource Description: $400-800 for families of SBISD students, employees, related individuals
Eligibility: Referral through SBISD CIS Caseworker. Aid given once yearly
Duplicate?: Yes, see Financial Assistance - Food and Rent/Mortgage and Utilities

Resource Description: $400-800 for families of SBISD students, employees, related individuals
Eligibility: Referral through SBISD CIS Caseworker. Aid given once yearly
Duplicate?: Yes, see Financial Assistance - Clothing and Rent/Mortgage and Utilities

Resource Description: Rental/Mortgage financial assistance for those who qualify
Eligibility: For St. Cecilia, zip codes 77024, 77043, 77055, 77080

Resource Description: $400-800 for families of SBISD students, employees, related individuals
Eligibility: Referral through SBISD CIS Caseworker. Aid given once yearly
Duplicate?: Yes, see financial Assistance - Food and Utilities

Resource Description: Rental/Mortgage financial assistance for those who qualify
Eligibility: For St. Cecilia, zip codes 77024, 77043, 77055, 77080

Resource Description: $400-800 for families of SBISD students, employees, related individuals
Eligibility: Referral through SBISD CIS Caseworker. Aid given once yearly

Resource Description: Furniture for those who need it
Eligibility: Referral and vouchers only through Chapelwood or other Furniture Bank Partners
Address: 8220 Mosely Rd. Houston, TX 77075 AND ALSO 5900 North Fwy #127 Houston, TX 77076
Hours: South - M-Sat 9-5 AND ALSO North - M-F 8-4
Application: Only through Chapelwood Serving Ministry or another participating partner

Resource Description: Very low-cost ESL classes for non-native speakers. Six instructional levels both in person and online. Three semesters per year.
Eligibility: MUST be 18 years or older, a resident within the Houston Metro area, not currently on probation for a violent crime. $40 application fee. Must complete the online application.
Address: MAM - 1625 Blalock Rd. Houston, TX 77080 but classes in various locations
Public Phone Number: (713) 266-8777 (for the ESL program) OR (713) 468-4516 for MAM's main switchboard
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.
Other Info: Classes offered on several campuses simultaneously

Resource Description: Very low-cost classes to help people prepare for their citizenship test and interview. 1. English/Civics class, 2. Civica en Español, 3. Interview Tutoring, and 4. Intensive Interview Tutoring
Eligibility: MUST be 18 years or older, a resident within the Houston Metro area, not currently on probation for a violent crime. $40 application fee. Must complete the online application.
Address: MAM - 1625 Blalock Rd. Houston, TX 77080 but classes in various locations
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.
Other Info: Classes offered online and in person

Resource Description: Two basic classes offered: Basic Excel and Basic Computer Skills for Seniors. More info on website.
Eligibility: Participants must have at least level 3 English. No age requirement, but expect to be with older adults. Must be a MAM client or student. Contact David Coles first. (See Other Info)
Address: MAM - 1625 Blalock Rd. Houston, TX 77080 but classes at Christ the King - 1201 Silber Rd.
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.
Other Info: Must contact David Coles to become a MAM client before registering.

Resource Description: Six-workshop course aimed at helping people succeed in the work environment
Eligibility: MUST attend an orientation session at MAM one day M-F at 10am. Arrive early, as capacity is 20 people. Must be functional in English, legally able to work in the US, have basic computer literacy, and be able to attend all workshops.
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.

Resource Description: Connect young adults with job training/ vocational training opportunities, career development plan, guidance, GED help, and other related skills
Eligibility: Must be 18-24 years old. For further eligibility requirements, attend a free online or in person orientation, M-F 10am. (see website)
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.
Other Info: Check out the Pathways map on the website for a clear explanation of just how cool this program is.

Resource Description: Assists clients to set job search goals, identify key skills and experiences, work on resumes, improve confidence, and find jobs.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.

Resource Description: Connecting people to vocational training in EKG Interpretation, CNA, Phlebotomy, and other careers
Eligibility: Participants must be MAM clients, at least 18 years old. Should attend orientation any day M-F 10am and complete the online forms. For more info, see website.
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.

Resource Name: Workforce ESL/Servicios de empleo en Espanol
Resource Description: Helps people working on their English find and keep great jobs.
Eligibility: Conversational level english, work authorized, motivated and willing to follow through.
Public Email: Amparo Arreaza: aarreaza@mamhouston.org OR Carolina Muraro: cmuraro@mamhouston.org
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.

Resource Name: Workforce ESL/Servicios de empleo en Espanol
Resource Description: Helps people working on their English find and keep great jobs.
Eligibility: Conversational level english, work authorized, motivated and willing to follow through.
Public Email: Amparo Arreaza: aarreaza@mamhouston.org OR Carolina Muraro: cmuraro@mamhouston.org
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.

Resource Description: Initial legal consultation for immigration services.
Eligibility: NO initial asylum applications or immigration court cases. Must schedule ahead of time online or by calling. Must make less than 400% of the federal poverty level. Must be here lawfully.

Resource Description: An overview of all the immigration services MAM offers on specific dates
Eligibility: Must be a green card holder (lawful permanent resident), 18 and up
Other Info: Services offered include: Citizenship/Naturalization Applications; DACA Renewals; Humanitarian-based petitions for victims of crime (U Visas); Adjustment of Status; Family Relative Petitions

Resource Description: Individual and family counseling for adults, children, and adolescents who meet screening requirements. Referrals given if needed.
Eligibility: Contact Eduardo Molina or Maria Espana for initial screening.

Resource Description: Free vaccines and other basic medical services
Eligibility: Eligibility varies based on program, but for Summer 2026, must be under 18 and have no health insurance, be covered by medicaid, or be underinsured or Native American or Alasa Native. Bring original printed shot record.
Other Info: Check back frequently, as their medical services change

Resource Description: Assistance enrolling in state and local benefits programs, especially SNAP, CHIP, Medicaid, Medicare QMB, TANF, Harris County Gold Card
Eligibility: No zip code restriction. They help people without computer skills, those unfamiliar with the process, and those who are undocumented but who have children who are US citizens and qualify for benefits. Must make an appointment
Walk-ins?: YES - but they'll make an appointment when you walk in

Resource Description: Material and financial assistance following a disaster or crisis
Eligibility: Case by case basis.
Walk-ins?: Yes, but it likely won't help.
Application: Check the website after big disasters

Resource Description: Material and financial assistance following a disaster or crisis
Eligibility: Case by case basis.
Walk-ins?: Yes, but it likely won't help.
Application: Check the website after big disasters

Resource Description: A program designed to help people stop living paycheck to paycheck. Lasts 5 months, once a week.
Eligibility: Internet access. Not in crisis. Relatives not participating in the same class. Commit to fully participate and complete all sessions. Must provide valid ID, current paystubs, complete application, interview.
Public Phone Number: ENGLISH - Jeanette Watkins (713) 489-6224 // SPANISH - Lucero Granados (713) 574-7534
Application: Contact the people listed for the full application.

Resource Description: Helps parents shop for new uniforms for their children using vouchers.
Eligibility: Must have a child in a school where CIS (Communities in Schools) is active and apply through them.
Application: Contact your child's CIS Student Support Manager

Resource Description: Assists parents in getting Christmas gifts for their children.
Eligibility: Must be an active MAM client and engaged in their services throughout the year. See website for more info.
Application: Contact MAM. Must be involved in their programs.

Resource Description: Integrated support services focusing on financial stability through increased career opportunities, community support, coaching, etc.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility

Resource Description: Financial assistance for those engaged in their programs
Eligibility: Must come in person and meet certain income guidelines. Must live within MAM's service area and give photo ID.
Walk-ins?: YES - they suggest coming in person
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Financial assistance for those engaged in their programs
Eligibility: Must come in person and meet certain income guidelines. Must live within MAM's service area and give photo ID.
Walk-ins?: YES - they suggest coming in person
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Clothing vouchers for those who qualify and come in person
Eligibility: Must come in person and meet certain income guidelines. Must live within MAM's service area and give photo ID.
Walk-ins?: YES - they suggest coming in person
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Single lunch bags, SNAP enrollment assistance, referrals to food pantries
Eligibility: Must come in person and meet certain income guidelines. Must live within MAM's service area and give photo ID.
Walk-ins?: YES - they suggest coming in person
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Gas vouchers, metro money to facilitate travel to work, job interviews, or medical appointments
Eligibility: Must come in person and meet certain income guidelines. Must live within MAM's service area and give photo ID.
Walk-ins?: YES - they suggest coming in person
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Tax return preparation and ITIN applications and renewals
Eligibility: Families/individuals earning up to $58,000/yr. Feb 2 through April 15 only. Must bring documents listed on the website.
Application: Go talk to them or call them.

Resource Description: Helping get small businesses off the ground with capital and resources in a cohort model
Eligibility: Small business owners seeking growth and expansion opportunities, established entrepreneurs, and individuals with existing businesses looking to enhance profitability. $50 fee to participate.
Application: Check back on the website periodically for new cohorts.

Resource Description: Year-round online and in-person classes aimed at financial literacy
Eligibility: Free to all
Application: No app needed, just register on the website

Resource Description: Coaches and personal guides who will work with individuals and families in their journey to financial stability
Eligibility: All individuals who are ready to start their own journey and willing to commit to work with a Navigator on their own goals
Public Phone Number: Several numbers on the website, but Laura Rodriguez - (713) 714-3687
Other Info: A note: in 2025 Fair Haven tried to get navigators on our campus full-time and it was decided our folks weren't at a level of stability commensurate with their services. Just FYI.

Resource Description: Helps eligible Texas families pay for child care with scholarships so families can go to work or attend job training.
Eligibility: Those who live in Texas, child under age 13 (or age 19 with a disability), child is a US citizen or has legal immigration status. Income limits as well - see site.

Resource Description: Helps eligible Texas families pay for child care with scholarships so families can go to work or attend job training.
Eligibility: Those who live in Texas, child under age 13 (or age 19 with a disability), child is a US citizen or has legal immigration status. Income limits as well - see site.

Resource Name: Rental Assistance, Assistance with Medical bills
Resource Description: Limited financial assistance for those who qualify
Eligibility: Must have lived in apartment for minimum four months. Household can receive assistance up to 3 times, once per 12 months. Check website for zip code restrictions - only serve south of I-10 on this side of town.
Application: Call during hours to set up an interview
Other Info: Keep calling back if the number is busy.

Resource Description: Limited financial assistance for those who qualify
Eligibility: Need based on sudden loss of income. Living in zip codes 77036, 77042, 77072, 77082, or 77099. Must have at least one child in residence under 16. Must currently owe less than $1000.
Address: 11169 Beechnut St. Ste G, Houston, TX 77072
Other Info: Application online

Resource Description: ADA-accessible shared ride paratransit service for those who qualify
Eligibility: Must fill out entire application, which requires a doctor's recommendation and online application proving inability to function with regular Metro services. For info on eligibility, click on Eligibility tab in website.
Languages: English, Spanish, and several others, though calls may only be in English or Spanish
Application: Scroll down and click on "How to Apply" https://www.ridemetro.org/riding-metro/transit-services/metrolift-paratransit

Resource Description: Shelter for youth with other related services aimed at getting youth to independence.
Eligibility: Ages 16-24 only.
Other Info: If they are full they will also help refer the young person somewhere else. They DO have transitional housing for these folks too.

Resource Description: Free bus tickets for young people ages 12-21 who have run away, been trafficked, or who need transportation to safe housing.
Eligibility: Youth ages 12-21 who are away from home and don't have a safe way to return who are ready to reunite with family or a safe adult who agrees to help. Must call.

Resource Description: Homes for ex-offenders offers a guided path back into society with varied 30-90 day programs for men or women.
Eligibility: Call for eligibility. In-person intake interviews by appointment. Limited walk-in availability. Beds are limited
Duplicate?: Yes - See also Housing - Transitional Housing - Felons

Resource Description: Homes for ex-offenders offers a guided path back into society with varied 30-90 day programs for men or women.
Eligibility: Call for eligibility. In-person intake interviews by appointment. Limited walk-in availability. Beds are limited

Organization Name: Harris County Resources for Children and Adults
Public Access?: Restricted - Outside (TRIAD, Community Youth Services, YMCA International Services, Texas Abuse Hotline, and others)
Resource Description: 24-bed emergency residential facility that provides short-term services to youth who are in need of shelter and care due to abuse, neglect, homelessness, or severe family conflict.
Eligibility: Must be in contact with one of the listed organizations and meet the criteria in the description.
Application: Must apply through one of the listed organizations
Other Info: Check website for numbers to referral organizations

Resource Description: Emergency and transitional housing for women and single families
Eligibility: Walk in with required documents (valid ID and SS card if available.) One bag per person. Must be able to care for yourself and not harm others.
Other Info: Wonderful continuum of care through transitional housing and personal development.

Resource Description: Emergency and transitional housing for men.
Eligibility: Must be a man who can care for themselves, are not harmful to themselves or others, can follow mision guidelines, don't have ankle monitors, aren't sex offenders.
Application: Walk in with required documents (valid ID and SS card if available)
Other Info: One bag per person. Intake can take 1-2 hours, but they provide sack lunches and water during this time. Wonderful continuum of care, but mixed reviews.

Resource Description: Residential program for moms and their kids that teaches them how to live independently and offers other services
Eligibility: Must be at least 18 years old. Maximum 4 children under 18 in full-time custody. No current open CPS cases. Not pregnant. No current drug use and 12 months sober from illegal substances. No criminal history. Must be working or in school. Must have driver's license and reliable transportation. Must have a good understanding of English.
Other Info: $50/month fee for the program.

Resource Description: Domestic violence shelter for women and children
Eligibility: Must contact one of the 24/7 hotlines
Application: Call Domestic Violence Hotline: 713-528-2121 OR Sexual Assault Hotline: 713-528-7273 OR TTY: 346-295-8994
Other Info: Their website is a wealth of information for ANYONE dealing with domestic violence or other related issues, not just women. Can also chat with a representative, etc.

Resource Description: Emergency shelter for veteran men.
Eligibility: Must be a male veteran.
Application: Call for questions. No good info on website.
Other Info: Veterans only.

Resource Description: Emergency shelter for women and families
Eligibility: Call for eligibility.
Application: Call for questions. No good info on website.
Other Info: Women and children only.

Resource Description: Career coaching, basic workforce training, job training, job placement for underserved communities.
Eligibility: Eligibility varies based on program. Call for eligibility information.
Application: Call for application OR check their online job board, depending on program.
Other Info: Brilliant jobs board here: https://sites.google.com/view/haul-wfdt-job-board/home

Organization Name: The Harris Center For Mental Health and IDD
Resource Description: 24/7 crisis hotline equipped to service acute mental health episodes.
Eligibility: Open to all.
Languages: English, Spanish, and several others, though calls may only be in English or Spanish
Other Info: For basic emotional support call 832-479-2135

Organization Name: The Harris Center For Mental Health and IDD
Resource Description: Text responders will provide basic support and community referral information.
Eligibility: Open to all. NOT for acute mental health issues or crisis situations.
Languages: English, Spanish, and likely other languages.
Other Info: NOTE: Text only. For crisis support, call 713-970-7000

Resource Description: Mental health crisis support available by call, text, chat, or video chat.
Eligibility: Open to all.
Languages: English and Spanish. Translation available through a language line for all others.

Resource Description: A safe place to get sober for the night and connect with resources for longer-term sobriety
Eligibility: Must be ambulatory, non-combative, 18 years of age or older, impaired on alcohol or other drugs. They do NOT admit individuals who are impaired from using bath salts or who have open wounds requiring stitches.
Address: 150 North Chenevert St. Ste 100 Houston, TX 77002
Other Info: Something isn't right about that number, but it's the best one I can find
Duplicate?: Yes, see Recovery - Detox and Sobering

Resource Description: A safe place to get sober for the night and connect with resources for longer-term sobriety
Eligibility: Must be ambulatory, non-combative, 18 years of age or older, impaired on alcohol or other drugs. They do NOT admit individuals who are impaired from using bath salts or who have open wounds requiring stitches.
Address: 150 North Chenevert St. Ste 100 Houston, TX 77002
Other Info: Something isn't right about that number, but it's the best one I can find
Duplicate?: Yes, see Medical Assistance - Detox and Sobering

Resource Description: Three tiers of support for those wanting to - or even considering - getting sober. Peer support all the way up to recovery coaches.
Eligibility: Must have come in contact with the Sobering Center operated by Houston Recovery Center
Address: 150 North Chenevert St. Ste 100 Houston, TX 77002
Other Info: Something isn't right about that number, but it's the best one I can find

Resource Description: Help learning about addiction, finding resources to address addiction, and getting into treatment. Knowledgeable people who will walk others through their options.
Eligibility: Varies by program.
Public Email: n/a but they do have a chat feature on the website

Resource Description: Free employment trainings in repair and maintenance, IT, manufacturing and logistics, and social services.
Eligibility: Must have a photo ID and phone number.
Other Info: The website has dozens of programs for different populations at different places across the city. Investigate what you want specifically first.

Resource Description: Who to call if you're not in immediate danger, but need to report minor crimes or have other less-urgent police assistance.
Eligibility: n/a
Other Info: For mental health concerns, contact 832-394-4200

Resource Description: Domestic violence shelter for ALL.
Eligibility: Must be a victim of domestic violence.
Address: Pasadena: 3811 Allen Genoa Rd., Pasadena, TX 77504 but they also have a Baytown office
Public Email: n/a but they do have a chat feature on the website
Other Info: Yes, they serve men.

Resource Description: Domestic violence hotline for all. Very knowledgeable and will forward callers to local resources.
Eligibility: Must be a victim of domestic violence, but even if you're not sure about that, they'll help you assess your situation.
Public Email: n/a, but text START to 88788 or chat online
Other Info: The website is a wealth of information about this too.

Resource Description: Drop-in facility for those facing homelessness. Many services can be accessed here.
Eligibility: Must be homeless or in threat of homelessness
Hours: General facility hours are Th-M 7am-3pm but vary by program
Other Info: Breakfast and hot homemade lunches Private showers Full-service laundry Device charging Access to phones and WiFi Mailbox privileges Resource & referral, basic law and housing things too.

Resource Description: Drop-in facility for those facing homelessness. Many services can be accessed here.
Eligibility: Must be homeless or in threat of homelessness
Other Info: Clothing and hygiene Center Mobile showers Social worker and health clinic Mailbox privileges Resource & referral, basic law and housing things too. Church services.

Resource Description: Who to call if you need immediate assistance for the safety and health of you and/or others.
Eligibility: n/a
Other Info: Be prepared for a long ordeal if you do this. Stay until the officers dismiss you, and seek legal council if you are uncertain about anything.

Organization Name: Harris County Center for Mental Health and IDD
Resource Description: For emergency psychiatric care and medication management
Eligibility: Bring ID and health insurance if you have any.
Public Email: There's a good chat feature on their website
Walk-ins?: Yes, but best to call ahead for availability
Other Info: If someone is opting into this, be prepared for it to take several hours. Contact any loved ones ahead of time to tell them what's happening, as they may take their phone away upon admission.

Resource Name: Emergency Center at Memorial City Medical Center
Resource Description: ER. Go here if someone is actively suicidal and likely a threat to themselves or others.
Eligibility: Bring ID and health insurance if you have any.
Other Info: If someone is opting into this, be prepared for it to take several hours. Contact any loved ones ahead of time to tell them what's happening, as they may take their phone away upon admission.

Resource Description: For emergency psychiatric care and medication management
Eligibility: Bring ID and health insurance if you have any.
Other Info: If someone is opting into this, be prepared for it to take several hours. Contact any loved ones ahead of time to tell them what's happening, as they may take their phone away upon admission.

Resource Description: Offers a variety of free and low-cost legal services, especially for those who are low-income.
Eligibility: Must meet financial and legal status eligibility requirements - usually no more than 125% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines
Hours: In-person office hours M-F 8am-5pm; Telephone intake hours M-Th 8:30am-2:30pm
Application: Apply online, by phone, or in-person. Online application here: https://www.lonestarlegal.org/get-help/
Other Info: They offer a LOT of services, among them eviction defense, fair housing, children's health and security, financial assistance and public benefits, legal assistance to survivors of sexual assault, and others.

Resource Description: Offers help with family, housing, taxes, wills, legal guardianship, veterans affairs, employment, and other legal matters
Eligibility: Take the assessment: https://legalhelphouston.org/

Resource Description: Low-cost or pro-bono legal assistance for almost anything
Eligibility: https://texaslawhelp.org/intake

Resource Description: Site that helps people with broken leases, evictions, and felonies find housing.
Eligibility: Fill out the screening form
Other Info: Should get a call back within 24 hours of filling out application

Resource Description: Site with info about navigating and avoiding eviction, along with how the process works.
Eligibility: n/a

Resource Description: Where to start if you don't know where to start. Broad guide to the social services of Texas
Eligibility: n/a
Public Email: n/a but they do have a chat feature on the website
Other Info: CALL them, or search the database on their website. Very useful.

Resource Description: Free food delivery in certain zip codes for kids under 5
Eligibility: Must have a child under 5 in the home, qualify for Medicaid, TANF, SNAP, or WIC, live in one of the approved zip codes, and fill out online application. Zip code map: https://kidsmealsinc.org/wp-content/uploads/55-Zip-Codes-5.pdf

Resource Description: Spring Branch-area food pantry
Eligibility: Need Picture ID and proof of current address. Might need SS or birth certificate for all members of the household

Resource Description: Very low-cost classes to help people prepare for their citizenship test and interview. 1. English/Civics class, 2. Civica en Español, 3. Interview Tutoring, and 4. Intensive Interview Tutoring
Eligibility: MUST be 18 years or older, a resident within the Houston Metro area, not currently on probation for a violent crime. $40 application fee. Must complete the online application.
Address: MAM - 1625 Blalock Rd. Houston, TX 77080 but classes in various locations
Walk-ins?: Yes, but they only talk to people M-Th 8:30-4:30 and F 8:30-1:30.
Other Info: Classes offered online and in person
Duplicate?: Yes, see Classes and Trainings - Citizenship

Resource Name: Memorial Drive Presbyterian Church Food Pantry
Resource Description: Food pantry with very limited availability
Eligibility: Open to everyone

Resource Name: Summer Feeding Meal Site - Boys and Girls Club - Spring Branch
Resource Description: PROGRAM DATES JUNE 8-JULY 24, 2026 ONLY. This site is a Houston Food Bank Summer Feeding Meal Site serving children ages 1–18. This site provides congregate (on-site) meals only to ensure children have access to nutritious meals during the summer.
Eligibility: Open to children ages 1-18. No registration, proof of income, or identification required. Meals must be consumed on-site in a congregate setting.

Resource Description: Santa Maria Hostel (SMH) is Texas’ largest multi-site gender-specific residential and outpatient substance abuse treatment center for females and one of a very few to provide a comprehensive continuum of care for pregnant and parenting women and their children.
Eligibility: Call for eligibility. Women with children call Vaughan Gilmore 713-957-2413. Single women call Donna White at 713-957-2413
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Name: Women's Recovery Residence and Community Center
Program Name (If Applicable): Women's Recovery Residence and Community Center
Resource Description: provides sober supportive housing for up to ten women and their children as well as providing space for the Women’s Recovery Community Center. The Community Center serves as a community resource for all women in recovery, providing space for support meetings, sober recreational activities, the Peer Advisory Council (PAC) alumni group, a resource center and recovery coaching.
Eligibility: Fill out the screening form and/or contact LaNisha Jiles at ljiles@santamariahostel.org
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Description: Those participating in the Recovery Housing Emerging Adults program engage in a peer oriented community by building strong connections with each other, while holding each other accountable. The program promotes long term recovery by providing a safe, sober living environment, using a social model of recovery, focusing on increasing a participant’s Recovery Capital, those personal, interpersonal and community resources that can be drawn upon to sustain addiction recovery.
Eligibility: Must be a woman between 18 and 25 or a woman with children and be in recovery from substance use.
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Name: STARS (Survivors of Trafficking Achieve Recovery and Stability)
Program Name (If Applicable): STARS (Survivors of Trafficking Achieve Recovery and Stability)
Resource Description: STARS provides housing along with substance use treatment and recovery support for survivors of human trafficking. STARS offers services including medical and mental health services, trauma-informed counseling, linkage to legal aid, and employment training or educational opportunities.
Eligibility: Contact Jared Price at jprice@santamariahostel.org and/or fill out the application
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Name: MIRRORS (Maternal Initiative for Reflective Recovery-Oriented Residential Services)
Program Name (If Applicable): MIRRORS (Maternal Initiative for Reflective Recovery-Oriented Residential Services)
Resource Description: The MIRRORS program provides integrated, family-focused medical and behavioral health services for current residential pregnant and postpartum clients, their children, and other family members. In partnership with Baylor College of Medicine, MIRRORS utilizes the Nurturing Parent and Celebrating Families curriculum and offers critical services like case management, family and child therapy, and Court Liaison services.
Eligibility: Contact Kendra Llorentz at 713-691-0900 or
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Name: COPSD (Co-Occurring Psychiatric and Substance Disorders Program)
Program Name (If Applicable): COPSD (Co-Occurring Psychiatric and Substance Disorders Program)
Resource Description: For clients battling co-occurring psychiatric and substance use disorders, the COPSD program provides integrated treatment to promote mental health stabilization.
Eligibility: Fill out the application
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Name: COPSD (Co-Occurring Psychiatric and Substance Disorders Program)
Program Name (If Applicable): COPSD (Co-Occurring Psychiatric and Substance Disorders Program)
Resource Description: For clients battling co-occurring psychiatric and substance use disorders, the COPSD program provides integrated treatment to promote mental health stabilization.
Eligibility: Fill out the application
Hours: Admissions is open 8:00am-5:00pm Monday through Friday.
Walk-ins?: No. Please complete the electronic screening application.

Resource Description: The mission of Turning Point Center is to help elderly homeless individuals – ages 50 or above – to get back on their feet and re-enter society as independent, self-sufficient, and productive citizens.
Eligibility: Must be 50+
Application: Call to apply or apply in person

Resource Name: Houston Immigration Legal Services Cooperative Immigration Rights Hotline
Resource Description: Free and low-cost immigration legal referrals, referrals to social services, immigration law policy updates, "know your rights" info, disaster and recovery resources.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: Not DOJ recognized. Free service.

Resource Name: Catholic Charities St. Francis Cabrini Center for Immigrant Legal Assistance
Program Name (If Applicable): St. Francis Cabrini Center for Immigrant Legal Assistance
Resource Description: legal assessment, citizenship application assistance, legal representation, advocacy, DACA Renewal Appointments, citizenship workshops, on-site consultation and information sessions called Charlas in the Galleria. Appt. required for that though. See website.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Address: 5599 San Felipe St. ste 300 Houston, TX 77056
Other Info: DOJ recognized. Free and low-cost options

Resource Description: Specifically for Vietnamese immigrants. Health awareness prevention programs, legal services for low-income families and victims of violence and crime, ESL, citizenship, and digital literacy classes, senior services programs to encourage community engagement, advocacy, and volunteerism
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: DOJ status unknown. Free and low-cost service options.

Resource Description: Citizenship and Lawful Permanent Residency; asylum; special immigrant juvenile status; removal defense cases; DACA; Visas for individuals who have survived a crime (U and T visas, VAWA docs); Temporary Protected Status; Work Authorization documents; "Know Your Rights" Presentations; Legal consultations;
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: DOJ recognized. Free.

Resource Description: Counseling and legal representation to individuals seeking immigration assistance, especially those in detention centers, asylum seekers, victims of domestic violence, crime victims, victims of human trafficking. Family reunification visas, adjustment of status, and naturalization applications. Refugee employment, resettlement. Assistance for trafficked persons.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Address: 6671 Southwest Freeway, Ste 250, Houston, TX 77074; but also 3110 Hayes Rd #300, Houston, TX 77082
Other Info: DOJ Recognized. Cost varies by program, but is affordable.

Resource Description: Legal and social services to immigrant women, girls, and survivors of gender-based violence. Free legal services, case management,
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: DOJ Recognized. Free services.

Resource Description: Variety of immigration services. See site for full list.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Address: 6200 Savoy Dr. Ste. 1200 Houston, TX 77036 OR 4410 Navigation Blvd. Houston, TX 77011
Other Info: Only the Savoy location is DOJ recognized. No info on fees.

Resource Name: Refugee and Immigrant Center for Education and Legal Services (RAICES)
Organization Name: Refugee and Immigrant Center for Education and Legal Services
Resource Description: Administrative and in-court legal services, bond payments, social services, refugee and client services, asylum services, child "know your rights" presentations.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Address: 1900 St James Pl, Suite 450 Houston, TX 77056
Other Info: DOJ recognized. $40 for a consultation.

Resource Description: Adjustment of Status, Asylum applications, Consular Processing, Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA), Employment authorization, Employment-based immigrant and non-immigrant petitions, Family-based petitions, NACARA, Naturalization/Citizenship, Removal hearings, T visas, Temporary Protected Status (TPS), U visas, Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) petitions
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Public Website: No official website. Google "CARECEN Houston" for best results.
Other Info: DOJ recognized. Small fee charged.

Resource Name: South Texas Pro Bono Asylum Representation Project (ProBAR)
Resource Description: Free and low-cost legal representation, especially for children. Help accessing social services, and more.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: DOJ status unknown. Free.

Resource Name: St. Jerome Catholic Church Food Distribution
Resource Description: Food pantry with limited offerings
Eligibility: Birth certificate or shot record required for each child and ID for all adults. Current rent or utility bill required.
Other Info: Call for most recent information

Resource Description: 2535 is a church-clinic under the care of Christ Clinic that aims to provide medical assistance to community members in need.
Eligibility: All are welcome, but they emphasize serving those without medical insurance or who are low income or have other barriers to access.
Other Info: SICK VISITS, HEALTH SCREENINGS, PREVENTATIVE CARE, CHRONIC DISEASE MANAGEMENT.

Resource Description: Distributes groceries, clothing, and hygiene essentials while also offering prayer and encouragement.
Eligibility: Both ID and Proof of address is required at the time of the appointment.
Other Info: NOTE: By appointment only. To make an appointment to receive food and clothes, please call 713.554.8820 Monday - Friday from 9a-2p leave a message with your full name and phone number. Families will be able to come 60 days in between each visit.

Resource Name: Summer Feeding Meal Site: Freed Park Community Center
Resource Description: ONLY JUNE 8-AUGUST 7, 2026. This site is a Houston Food Bank Summer Feeding Meal Site serving children ages 1–18. This site provides congregate (on-site) meals only to ensure children have access to nutritious meals during the summer.
Eligibility: Children ages 1-18. No registration or identification is required. Meals must be consumed on-site in a congregate setting.
Public Phone Number: Text FOOD to 304304 to find this and other sites
Other Info: There are more sites than are listed here. Text FOOD to 304304 to find other sites.

Resource Description: Inpatient treatment for women with several levels of support and a continuum of care upon completion.
Eligibility: See website for program eligibility. Several conditions.
Other Info: This program is free.

Resource Description: IOP with group sessions and individual support to address substance use disorder concerns.
Eligibility: Must live in the greater Houston area, currently have a substance use disorder or co-occurring disorder, and be willing and able to fully participate in an intensive outpatient treatment program.
Application: See website for client interest form.
Other Info: This program is free.

Resource Description: Permanent supportive housing for homeless and vulnerable individuals. Offers several services including substance abuse treatment, food and transport resources, and others.
Eligibility: Contact for eligibility
Other Info: Next to Mabee WholeLife service center. For families, try Adele and Ber Pieper. Not an emergency shelter.

Program Name (If Applicable): Adele and Ber Pieper Family Place
Resource Description: Permanent supportive housing for families with income no greater than 60% of median family income. Offers two on-site case managers and several resources. *Half of these units can be accessed only through Coalition for the Homeless and Houston Housing Authority
Eligibility: Families with income no greater than 60% of median family income.
Other Info: Not an emergency shelter. For individuals, try Jane Cizik

Resource Description: Where to look when you need an AA meeting in the Houston area. This site also connects to Intergroup for non-Houston meetings
Eligibility: None, though check the open/closed status and demographics of each meeting to make sure you're going to one where you'll be welcome. (Don't go to a women's meeting if you're a man, for example.) Check out this page if you're not sure if AA is for you: https://aahouston.org/resources/is-aa-for-you/
Public Phone Number: Call 713-686-6300 to find a meeting and get help
Languages: English, Spanish. Meetings offered in any language you'd want if you're willing to look.

Resource Description: Where to look when you need an Al-Anon or Alateen meeting anywhere in the world. Meetings offered online, in-person, and over the phone
Eligibility: Al-Anon meetings are meant to be attended by those dealing with the reality of a loved one in addiction, but no one is going to check you. If you're not sure if it's for you, go to this page: https://al-anon.org/newcomers/self-quiz/
Public Phone Number: See the website for telephone meetings.
Languages: English, Spanish. Meetings offered in any language you'd want if you're willing to look.

Resource Name: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous Meeting Finder
Resource Description: Where to look when you need an SLAA meeting in Houston. SLAA seems to be more locally organized, so while you'll be able to find in-person meetings here for Houston, each locality is going to have its own in-person meeting structure. PLEASE NOTE SLAA meetings are very often single-gender. Online meetings can be found here: https://slaafws.org/meetings/
Eligibility: All SLAA meetings are "closed" meaning the requirement for attendance is that you have a desire to stop acting out on an SLAA pattern of behavior. This page will help you determine if SLAA is right for you: https://slaa-houston.org/newcomers
Languages: English, though worldwide and online meetings are offered in other languages.

Organization Name: Houston Area Service Committee of NA
Resource Description: Where to look to find NA meetings in the greater Houston area. For meetings outside of the Houston area and for more virtual meetings, go here: https://na.org/MeetingSearch/
Eligibility: This page will help you determine if NA is right for you: https://hascona.com/who-is-an-addict/
Languages: English, Spanish. Other languages offered if you're willing to look.

Resource Description: Where to look to find an OA meeting near you.
Eligibility: Take this quiz to see if OA is right for you: https://oa.org/a-place-to-begin/quiz/

Resource Description: A variety of support groups for primarily non-recovery-related issues. Topics include Crohn's Disease, Grief support, NAMI, depression and bipolar support, and others.
Eligibility: Read group descriptions on the website to determine eligibility

Resource Description: Support for those caring for loved ones with physical and mental health issues, special needs, or other issues.
Eligibility: Read group descriptions on the website to determine eligibility
